text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST AND MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST
Protocol-Mandated Restaging Examination — Phase II Clinical Trial

CLINICAL INDICATION:
64-year-old female with stage IV (M1c) EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma, right lower lobe primary. Relevant treatment history: initially treated with osimertinib for approximately 14 months with disease progression documented by growth of the right lower lobe primary, new adrenal metastasis, new bilateral pulmonary nodules, and three new intracranial metastases; acquired EGFR T790M resistance mutation confirmed at time of progression. Subsequently received stereotactic radiosurgery (Gamma Knife) to three brain metastases (right cerebellar, left frontal, right parietal), followed by four cycles of carboplatin/pemetrexed (carboplatin dose-reduced to AUC 4 after cycle 2 due to thrombocytopenia) and then two cycles of pemetrexed maintenance. Currently on phase II investigational clinical trial: EGFR/MET bispecific monoclonal antibody (intravenous, every 2 weeks) plus oral SOS1 inhibitor (daily, 28-day cycle). Now approximately 8 weeks on study (2 cycles completed). Protocol-mandated restaging. Comparison: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed approximately 10 weeks prior (during pemetrexed maintenance); MRI brain performed approximately 20 weeks prior (at time of 3-cycle platinum-doublet reassessment).

TECHNIQUE:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS: Multidetector CT acquired from the lung apices through the symphysis pubis following administration of 100 mL of iohexol contrast intravenously. Arterial and portal venous phase imaging of the abdomen and pelvis. Multiplanar reconstructions performed. DLP: 1,248 mGy·cm.

MRI BRAIN: 3.0 Tesla MRI brain performed with and without gadolinium contrast (gadobutrol 0.1 mmol/kg IV). Sequences obtained: axial T1 pre- and post-contrast, axial T2 FLAIR, axial diffusion-weighted imaging/ADC, axial T2*, coronal T1 post-contrast, and 3D volumetric T1 post-contrast (MPRAGE). No contraindication to gadolinium noted.

--- CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS ---

FINDINGS:

LUNGS AND AIRWAYS:
The central airways are patent. The right lower lobe mass, previously measuring 2.3 cm on the most recent prior examination, now measures approximately 1.7 cm in its greatest transaxial dimension. The lesion retains a spiculated morphology but appears somewhat less conspicuous and shows decreased soft tissue attenuation compared with prior studies, which may reflect internal treatment effect. No new satellite nodules are identified surrounding the primary lesion. The bilateral pulmonary nodules previously identified (largest 8 mm right upper lobe on the initial progression scan, subsequently stable on maintenance imaging) are no longer visualized — no discrete nodular densities identified to correspond to those prior findings. There are no new pulmonary nodules identified. No endobronchial lesion. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. Mild fibrotic changes in the right lower lobe parenchyma adjacent to the primary lesion, consistent with post-treatment sequelae and/or associated desmoplastic change.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes by size criteria. A small right paratracheal (station 4R) lymph node measures up to 7 mm in short axis, decreased in size compared to the 12 mm node documented at systemic progression on osimertinib. No new nodal stations involved. No pericardial effusion. Heart size within normal limits. No superior vena cava compression or distortion.

CHEST WALL AND PLEURA:
No pleural effusion bilaterally. No chest wall invasion or osseous destruction. Mild degenerative changes of the visualized thoracic spine.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: Normal hepatic morphology. No focal hepatic lesions. Portal and hepatic venous anatomy unremarkable. No biliary ductal dilatation.
Spleen: Unremarkable, normal size and attenuation.
Pancreas: Unremarkable. No pancreatic ductal dilatation.
Adrenal glands: The right adrenal gland contains a nodule that now measures approximately 0.7 cm in greatest dimension, decreased from 1.1 cm on the most recent prior CT. The lesion is well-defined, with soft tissue density consistent with prior biopsy-confirmed metastasis. No new bilateral adrenal lesion identified.
Kidneys: Both kidneys enhance symmetrically without hydronephrosis or focal mass lesion. A simple-appearing 8 mm right renal cyst is unchanged.
Bowel: No bowel obstruction. No free air or free fluid.
Retroperitoneum/Lymph nodes: No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymph nodes.
Vasculature: Aorta and major branches of normal caliber without aneurysm.

PELVIS:
Uterus and adnexa: No pelvic mass. Uterus unremarkable for age. No suspicious pelvic lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable. No free pelvic fluid.

BONES (LIMITED EVALUATION ON SOFT TISSUE WINDOWS):
No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions identified on the visualized skeleton to suggest bony metastatic disease. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine and bilateral hips within expected range for age.

--- MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST ---

FINDINGS:

KNOWN TREATED METASTATIC LESIONS:
(1) Right cerebellar hemisphere lesion (treated with 20 Gy Gamma Knife): On current examination, a small focus of susceptibility artifact and peripheral enhancement is present, now measuring approximately 0.4 cm, decreased from 0.6 cm on prior MRI brain. There is central signal loss on susceptibility sequences consistent with radiation necrosis/hemosiderin deposition. The previously noted surrounding edema has resolved. No appreciable mass effect on the fourth ventricle.
(2) Left frontal lobe lesion (treated with 22 Gy Gamma Knife): This lesion now appears as a tiny focus of T1 post-contrast signal, measuring approximately 0.3 cm, decreased from 0.4 cm on prior MRI. Surrounding T2/FLAIR signal abnormality has diminished substantially. No new enhancement surrounding this site.
(3) Right parietal lobe lesion (treated with 24 Gy Gamma Knife): This focus has further decreased and is now at the limit of reliable measurement at approximately 0.2 cm, representing near-complete response at this site. No surrounding edema.

NEW LESIONS:
No new enhancing intracranial lesions identified. Careful review of the 3D MPRAGE post-contrast sequences does not reveal any additional foci of abnormal enhancement that would be suspicious for new metastatic deposits.

LEPTOMENINGEAL DISEASE:
No abnormal leptomeningeal enhancement identified. Sulcal and cisternal spaces are patent.

BRAIN PARENCHYMA:
No areas of restricted diffusion to suggest acute infarction. No midline shift. The ventricles are normal in caliber and configuration. No mass effect. No hydrocephalus. Mild periventricular white matter T2/FLAIR signal changes in keeping with chronic small vessel ischemic disease, unchanged from prior. No new signal abnormality in the brainstem or cerebellum outside the known treated site.

SELLA AND SKULL BASE:
Unremarkable. No parasellar or skull base lesion.

CALVARIUM AND ORBITS:
No calvarial metastatic disease. Orbits unremarkable bilaterally.

IMPRESSION:

1. RIGHT LOWER LOBE PRIMARY: The spiculated right lower lobe mass has decreased in size, now measuring approximately 1.7 cm compared to 2.3 cm on prior imaging, consistent with ongoing treatment response to current clinical trial therapy.

2. BILATERAL PULMONARY NODULES: The previously identified small bilateral pulmonary nodules are no longer visualized, representing interval resolution.

3. RIGHT ADRENAL METASTASIS: The biopsy-confirmed right adrenal nodule has decreased from 1.1 cm to approximately 0.7 cm, consistent with treatment response at this site.

4. MEDIASTINAL LYMPH NODES: The previously enlarged right paratracheal lymph node (4R) has further decreased and is now sub-centimeter, with no pathologically enlarged mediastinal nodal stations identified.

5. INTRACRANIAL DISEASE: The three previously treated intracranial metastases (right cerebellar, left frontal, right parietal) show continued and progressive volume reduction with features of evolving radiation necrosis, in keeping with durable response to prior stereotactic radiosurgery. No new intracranial metastatic lesions. No leptomeningeal involvement.

6. No new sites of disease identified in the chest, abdomen, pelvis, or brain.

Overall, the findings across all assessed sites are consistent with a favorable and ongoing treatment response to the current investigational regimen.

Reporting radiologist attestation: Images personally reviewed and interpreted by the attending radiologist.
